title: rosa rubiginosa subsp. aciculata
intro: Rosa rubiginosa subsp. aciculata, also known as the alpine sweetbriar, is a subspecies of the fragrant rose plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and menstrual problems, due to its high content of fragrant oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa subsp. aciculata include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
